comment: Mixed fixed-income book for Convex vs QuantLib reconciliation. Every identifier is real and traceable unless explicitly marked as synthetic. See README.md for source citations and known limits.

instruments.description: US Treasury 10Y note, on-the-run as of 2025-12-31
US Treasury 5Y note issued Jan 2023, 2Y remaining on valuation date. Exercises short-dated pricing.
US Treasury 2Y FRN indexed to 13-week T-Bill high discount rate (bond-equivalent), spread 19bps. Daily index accrual, quarterly payment.
instruments.index_rate_note: Late-2025 13W T-Bill placeholder; live pull from FRED DGS3MO would replace this. All future coupons projected at a flat (index + spread) rate for the reconciliation.
instruments.note: UST FRN market convention is ACT/360 simple accrual. For this reconciliation we project all future coupons at (index + spread) and use ACT/ACT ICMA — period-based — as the day count. Rationale: under ACT/360, QL varies each quarterly coupon with actual-days/360 while Convex's FixedRateBond uses rate/freq uniformly (the semi-annual ACT/ACT ICMA case they were built for). ICMA on both sides produces rate/freq exactly, so the reconciliation tests PV/risk under quarterly conventions without hitting that design difference.

instruments.description: US Treasury 10Y TIPS. Real coupon 1.875%, principal indexed to CPI-U with 3-month lag.
instruments.note: Index ratio on valuation date must be pulled at runtime (see pull_market_data.py). Index ratio at issue = 1.00108.

instruments.description: 4.75% Treasury Gilt 2035. On-the-run 10Y UK gilt benchmark.
2.50% Bundesanleihe 2025/2035. Annual coupon — tests non-semi-annual path.
Apple 4.65% notes due 2046. Bullet structure with make-whole redemption language.

make_whole.note: Exact spread in 424B2 — pull from https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/320193/000119312516463982/d60003d424b2.htm. For reconciliation purposes treat as bullet (make-whole is deep OTM for any reasonable rate scenario).

instruments.description: Microsoft 3.50% notes due 2035. Bullet with make-whole.

make_whole.note: Spread is in the FWP (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/789019/000119312515040186/d868361dfwp.htm). Treat as bullet for reconciliation.

instruments.description: Verizon 4.329% notes due 2028. Senior unsecured with make-whole.

make_whole.note: Spread to be pulled from Verizon 424B on EDGAR (CIK 732712). Treat as bullet for reconciliation.

instruments.description: Ford Credit 6.798% notes due 2028-11-07. Make-whole at UST+35bps until 2028-10-07 then par-call until maturity. Fully verified from 424B2 snippet.
instruments.cusip_note: CUSIP not captured in search snippet; pull from the 424B2 or from Ford Credit's outstanding-debt schedule.

instruments.description: Clearly-labeled synthetic HY bond exercising the multi-date step-down code path. Industry-standard 4yr-NC-then-step-down structure. Terms chosen so QuantLib CallableFixedRateBond and Convex can both ingest the schedule.
instruments.source: Synthetic — standard HY 4yr-NC + step-down template. Not a real bond.
instruments.rationale: Real HY indentures were behind WebFetch 403s. Three candidate SEC exhibits identified (T-Mobile 3.875% 2030, Occidental 8.875% 2030, Bausch 11% 2028). To avoid fabricating a real bond's call grid, use a clearly synthetic instrument with template structure.

instruments.description: Clearly-labeled synthetic 5Y Bermudan puttable bond. Holder may put at par on each of 2026-01-15, 2027-01-15, 2028-01-15. Exercises CallableBond.with_put_schedule + multi-put YTB / workout-bullet path on both libraries.
instruments.source: Synthetic — standard FHLB-style annual European put strip. Not a real CUSIP.
instruments.rationale: FHLB putable schedules sit behind a search-form gate (no programmatic feed in this session). The schedule here mirrors the typical agency Bermudan put structure (par-only, annual, post-lockout). Replace with a real CUSIP once a primary-source schedule is digitized.

instruments.description: Clearly-labeled synthetic 10Y plain sinking-fund bond exercising the SinkingFundBond / ql.AmortizingFixedRateBond paths. Annual paydown of 20% on each anniversary 2026-12-15 through 2030-12-15. Coupons accrue on outstanding face.
instruments.source: Synthetic — standard 5y-NCEU plain sinker template. Not a real CUSIP.
instruments.rationale: IBRD/IADB don't issue public USD plain sinkers (bullets only). Real corporate/agency sinkers (FHLB, AT&T, utility) require manual prospectus extraction; the schedule here mirrors industry-standard templates. Replace with a real CUSIP once a primary-source schedule is digitized.

instruments.description: Clearly-labeled synthetic 5Y callable SOFR FRN. Coupon = compounded SOFR + 125bps, quarterly ACT/360. Issuer may call at par on each anniversary 2027-11-15 through 2029-11-15. Anchor is mid-month rather than quarter-end so both 2025-12-31 and 2025-06-30 snapshots fall mid-period — exercises ARRC compound-in-arrears × workout-bullet truncation on both libraries.
instruments.source: Synthetic — standard bank-capital callable SOFR FRN template (NC2 + annual Bermudan). Not a real CUSIP.
instruments.rationale: Most real callable SOFR FRNs (FHLB callable advances, bank Tier-2 sub-debt) sit behind FHLB search forms or 144A indentures. The schedule here mirrors the typical NC2-annual structure. Replace with a real CUSIP once a primary-source schedule is digitized.

instruments.description: US Treasury 52-Week Bill, auctioned 2025-12-23, issued 2025-12-26, matures 2026-12-24. Pure zero-coupon discount instrument; exercises ZeroCouponBond + ACT/360 path on both libraries.
instruments.auction_source: TreasuryDirect auction announcement A_20251218_4.pdf (52-week Bill, auctioned 2025-12-23)
